programmes.show_description: One Guilty Deed: Another case for the British detective duo who sprang from the mind of a top US crime writer. Lynley and Havers find a sinister underworld in a seaside town.
Time Decays: An idyllic Shakespeare walk soon turns to disaster when one of the walkers is murdered and Frank becomes the prime suspect.
Caring: Comedy with the Essex sisters and their oversexed neighbour. When Sharon and Tracey's aunt comes to convalesce, the sisters discover she can be very demanding.
New. The Saloon Killer: Part 2: The Saloon Shooter mows down two new victims in Phoswa Village. Meanwhile, Micki presents her course in Cape Town, where she also reunites with AJ.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Keeping Up Appearances: No, not Mrs Bucket - Sharon and Tracey! Tracey is shocked to find that two years in prison have finally taken their toll on Darryl.
Tinker, Tailor: Comedy from the famed Essex siblings. Sharon begins to wonder if her husband was right when he told her there was a 'grass' planted in his cell.
Highland Fling - Part Two: Second in a two-parter of the much-loved drama. Lovejoy tries to help the castle owners avoid foreclosure. Eric finds a valuable mirror.
The Napoleonic Commode: Popular comic drama series. The bailiffs have finally caught up with our hero and his only salvation appears to be a Napoleonic commode. Ian McShane stars.
The Ring: Lighthearted drama with the maverick antiques dealer. Lovejoy tricks Lady Jane into taking part in an illegal bidding ring at an auction - threatening their relationship.
The Angel of Mercy: Mrs McCarthy's friend is the latest villager to die in her sleep, and Father Brown soon senses something sinister is afoot. Fifties-set drama, starring Mark Williams.
Lost in Translation: Another gruelling case for James Bolam and the experienced ex-police officers. The team investigate a crucified body found near Waltham Forest in 1996.
Setting Out Your Stall: The hugely popular crime drama series with a cast of the nation's favourites. The UCOS team investigate the death of a market trader, Kathy Green.
Ghost's Forge: Comic actor and QI regular Alan Davies stars with Caroline Quentin in the quirky mystery drama. Jonathan investigates the motiveless murder of an elderly hermit.
An Appetite for Murder: Part One: When a popular but tempestuous celebrity chef is murdered during his cookbook launch, there's no shortage of suspects for Judith, Suzie and Becks.
An Appetite for Murder: Part Two: As police investigate the chef's death, a new development makes them rethink everything they have learned so far. Guest starring Susan Lynch.
Brokenwood-o-saurus: With dinosaur fossils discovered in the foothills of Brokenwood, the town is buzzing. But when a palaeontologist is found dead, the future doesn't look so rosy.
A connection to a 16th-century murder seems to confirm someone is killing witches in Whitechapel, but the team fears a malign influence is at work.
Word of God: Suspenseful crime drama with upper-crust DI Thomas Lynley and DS Barbara Havers. The duo are called to Smithfield Market, where a frozen body has been found.
Lost in Translation: Another gruelling case for James Bolam and the experienced ex-police officers. The team investigate a crucified body found near Waltham Forest in 1996.
Pilot: Miep Gies agrees to help Otto Frank hide in the secret annexe of his business premises in Amsterdam, along with members of the van Pels and Pfeffer families.
Tinker, Tailor: Comedy from the famed Essex siblings. Sharon begins to wonder if her husband was right when he told her there was a 'grass' planted in his cell.
Sparring with Death: First Chapter: When Mattie investigates a murder at Wildemarsh Wellness Retreat, she finds several people with unhealthy intentions towards each other.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
When the Snow Lay Round About: It's two nights before Christmas, and the whole of Sun Hill is desperately hoping there won't be a shout. Pretty unlikely!
Blowing It All Away: PC Rickman becomes increasingly friendly with a drug user and soon comes under suspicion of using drugs herself.
All Change: Sun Hill's CID join the uniform beat on the biggest night of the century - the eve of the new millennium.
In the Firing Line: Smith goes undercover as a contract killer in a drug-dealing feud, but his target is a little too close for comfort.
Thug on the Tyne: Part 1: Frank Burnside goes away from the manor as he asks for Rawton's help in a major undercover operation in Newcastle.
A new midwife joins the team as the area is rocked by power cuts and blizzards, but she falls ill as soon as she arrives and soon has to assist with a difficult birth.
Lucille is on duty when a friend of Valerie's goes into labour - but when the patient's health deteriorates, the new recruit is accused of incompetence by her racist mother.
Atmospheric, gripping feature-length drama set in Victorian Newcastle, adapted from the best-seller. Orphaned Millie is taken in by a local rag woman, and a new life begins.
My Brother's Keeper: Sister Reginald's ex-con brother Alfie shows up at the convent. The sisters agree to let him stay, but when an artist is found murdered, all eyes are on Alfie.
Look to the Lady - Part One: Fifth 'Doctor Who' Peter Davison's next role was the mysterious 1930s detective in this gripping series. Campion battles to save a relic from art thieves.
Heads, I Win. Tails, You Lose.: Harry and Fergus take on a disturbing case - a serial killer targeting random victims and leaving a coin in their mouths. Heads, I Win. Tails, You Lose.
In Plain Sight: Emilia Fox stars in a feature-length forensics mystery. The accidental fatal shooting of a Polish teenager puts a firearms division under immense public scrutiny.
